name: AI/ML Architecture Ontology
description: Vendor-neutral AI/ML architecture ontology covering AI workloads, autonomous agents, RAG systems, ML pipelines, model registries, agent orchestration, architecture patterns, and responsible AI controls. Extracted from EA-MSFT-ONT to enable multi-cloud AI architecture modelling across Azure, AWS, GCP, and open-source platforms.

oaa:imports.name: EA-CORE-ONT
oaa:imports.description: Platform-agnostic EA core providing Application, TechnologyArchitecture, SecurityArchitecture base types for AI workload hosting and governance

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Application-to-AIWorkload
oaa:joinPatterns.description: EA-CORE applications host or integrate AI workloads for intelligent capabilities
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: AI workload portfolio mapping and application intelligence assessment

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Agent-to-Application Tools
oaa:joinPatterns.description: AI agents use EA-CORE applications as tools for autonomous task execution
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Agent tool inventory and application dependency mapping

oaa:joinPatterns.name: ResponsibleAI-to-Security
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Responsible AI controls integrate with security architecture for governance
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: AI governance and security posture alignment

entities.name: AIWorkload
entities.description: An application leveraging AI/ML capabilities for inference, generation, or decision-making. AIWorkloads are vendor-neutral and can be hosted on any cloud platform (Azure AI, AWS SageMaker, GCP Vertex AI, open-source). AIWorkloads implement architecture patterns and are governed by Responsible AI controls.

oaa:properties.name: modelProvider
oaa:properties.description: AI model provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Meta, Mistral, open-source)

oaa:properties.name: deploymentPlatform
oaa:properties.description: Cloud or on-premises platform hosting the workload

entities.name: AIAgent
entities.description: An autonomous AI agent with tool-use capabilities, powered by large language models. Agents can use tools, maintain memory, plan multi-step actions, and collaborate with other agents in multi-agent orchestration patterns. Vendor-neutral — supports Azure AI Agent Service, AWS Bedrock Agents, Anthropic Claude, LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen, and custom frameworks.

oaa:properties.name: agentFramework
oaa:properties.description: Agent framework (LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen, Semantic Kernel, custom)

entities.name: RAGSystem
entities.description: A Retrieval-Augmented Generation system that combines document retrieval with language model generation. RAG systems use vector stores (Pinecone, Weaviate, pgvector, Azure AI Search, Elasticsearch) for semantic search and LLMs for response synthesis. Supports basic, advanced, and agentic RAG patterns.

oaa:properties.name: vectorStore
oaa:properties.description: Vector database used for embeddings (Pinecone, Weaviate, pgvector, FAISS, Azure AI Search)

oaa:properties.name: chunkingStrategy
oaa:properties.description: Document chunking approach (fixed-size, semantic, recursive, parent-child)

entities.name: MLPipeline
entities.description: A machine learning pipeline for model training, evaluation, and deployment. Covers data preprocessing, feature engineering, model training, hyperparameter tuning, evaluation, and deployment stages. Vendor-neutral — supports Azure ML, SageMaker, Vertex AI, MLflow, Kubeflow, and custom pipelines.

oaa:properties.name: pipelineStages
oaa:properties.description: Pipeline stages (ingest, preprocess, train, evaluate, deploy, monitor)

oaa:properties.name: orchestrator
oaa:properties.description: Pipeline orchestration tool (Azure ML, SageMaker, Kubeflow, Airflow, MLflow)

oaa:properties.name: computeTarget
oaa:properties.description: Compute resource for training (GPU cluster, serverless, on-prem)

entities.name: ModelRegistry
entities.description: A centralised repository for versioned ML models, model cards, lineage tracking, and deployment metadata. Supports governance through approval workflows and audit trails. Vendor-neutral — covers Azure ML Model Registry, SageMaker Model Registry, MLflow Model Registry, and Weights & Biases.

oaa:properties.name: registryPlatform
oaa:properties.description: Registry platform (Azure ML, SageMaker, MLflow, W&B, custom)

oaa:properties.name: governanceLevel
oaa:properties.description: Model governance maturity (None, Basic, Managed, Enterprise)

entities.name: AgentOrchestrator
entities.description: A coordination layer for multi-agent systems that manages agent selection, task delegation, message routing, and workflow orchestration. Supports patterns like supervisor, consensus, pipeline, and hierarchical delegation. Vendor-neutral — covers AutoGen, CrewAI, LangGraph, Semantic Kernel, and custom orchestrators.

entities.name: AIArchitecturePattern
entities.description: A reusable AI solution architecture pattern (Basic RAG, Advanced RAG, Single Agent, Multi-Agent, Copilot, Fine-Tuning, Batch Inference) with defined components and vendor-neutral implementation guidance. Patterns can be implemented on any cloud platform.

entities.name: ResponsibleAIControl
entities.description: A control for responsible and ethical AI use covering content safety, groundedness verification, jailbreak protection, PII redaction, bias detection, and model transparency. Vendor-neutral — aligns to NIST AI RMF, EU AI Act, ISO 42001, OWASP LLM Top 10, and MITRE ATLAS.

oaa:properties.name: regulatoryAlignment
oaa:properties.description: Regulatory frameworks this control aligns to (NIST AI RMF, EU AI Act, ISO 42001)

relationships.name: hostsWorkload
relationships.description: An EA-CORE application hosts or integrates an AI workload for intelligent capabilities

relationships.name: usesModel
relationships.description: An AI workload uses one or more AI/ML models for inference, generation, or decision-making

relationships.name: hasTools
relationships.description: An AI agent has tools (applications, APIs, functions) available for autonomous task execution

relationships.name: implementsPattern
relationships.description: An AI workload implements a specific AI architecture pattern (RAG, Agent, Copilot, etc.)

relationships.name: securedByControl
relationships.description: An AI workload is secured by Responsible AI controls ensuring ethical, safe, and compliant AI deployment

relationships.name: orchestrates
relationships.description: An agent orchestrator coordinates multiple AI agents in a multi-agent system

relationships.name: trainedByPipeline
relationships.description: An AI workload's model is trained or fine-tuned by an ML pipeline

relationships.name: registeredIn
relationships.description: An AI workload's model is registered in a model registry for versioning and governance

relationships.name: alignsToSecurity
relationships.description: A Responsible AI control aligns to the enterprise security architecture for integrated governance

hasDefinedTerm.name: BasicRAG
hasDefinedTerm.description: Standard retrieval-augmented generation

hasDefinedTerm.name: AdvancedRAG
hasDefinedTerm.description: RAG with query rewriting, reranking, multi-step retrieval

hasDefinedTerm.name: AgenticRAG
hasDefinedTerm.description: RAG with autonomous agent-driven retrieval and reasoning

hasDefinedTerm.name: SingleAgent
hasDefinedTerm.description: Autonomous tool-using agent with single LLM

hasDefinedTerm.name: MultiAgent
hasDefinedTerm.description: Collaborative specialised agents with orchestration

hasDefinedTerm.name: Copilot
hasDefinedTerm.description: Embedded AI assistant in business application

hasDefinedTerm.name: FineTuning
hasDefinedTerm.description: Custom model training for domain-specific use

hasDefinedTerm.name: BatchInference
hasDefinedTerm.description: Asynchronous large volume data processing

enumerations.name: ResponsibleAICategory
enumerations.description: Categories of Responsible AI controls aligned to NIST AI RMF, EU AI Act, and ISO 42001

hasDefinedTerm.name: ContentSafety
hasDefinedTerm.description: Filter harmful or inappropriate content

hasDefinedTerm.name: GroundednessCheck
hasDefinedTerm.description: Verify responses are grounded in source documents

hasDefinedTerm.name: JailbreakProtection
hasDefinedTerm.description: Prevent prompt injection and jailbreak attempts

hasDefinedTerm.name: PIIRedaction
hasDefinedTerm.description: Detect and redact personally identifiable information

hasDefinedTerm.name: ModelTransparency
hasDefinedTerm.description: Document model capabilities, limitations, intended use

hasDefinedTerm.name: BiasDetection
hasDefinedTerm.description: Detect and mitigate algorithmic bias in model outputs

hasDefinedTerm.name: Explainability
hasDefinedTerm.description: Provide interpretable explanations for AI decisions

hasDefinedTerm.name: RealTime
hasDefinedTerm.description: Synchronous low-latency inference

hasDefinedTerm.name: Batch
hasDefinedTerm.description: Asynchronous high-throughput batch processing

hasDefinedTerm.name: Streaming
hasDefinedTerm.description: Token-by-token streaming inference

enumerations.name: AutonomyLevel
enumerations.description: Degree of autonomous operation for AI agents

hasDefinedTerm.name: Assisted
hasDefinedTerm.description: Human-in-the-loop for all decisions

hasDefinedTerm.name: SemiAutonomous
hasDefinedTerm.description: Agent acts independently within defined boundaries, human approval for escalations

hasDefinedTerm.name: Autonomous
hasDefinedTerm.description: Fully autonomous operation with post-hoc human review

hasDefinedTerm.name: Basic
hasDefinedTerm.description: Simple retrieve-then-generate with single vector lookup

hasDefinedTerm.name: Advanced
hasDefinedTerm.description: Multi-step retrieval with reranking and query expansion

hasDefinedTerm.name: Agentic
hasDefinedTerm.description: Agent-driven adaptive retrieval with tool use

enumerations.name: OrchestrationPattern
enumerations.description: Multi-agent orchestration patterns for agent coordination

hasDefinedTerm.name: Supervisor
hasDefinedTerm.description: Central supervisor agent delegates to specialist agents

hasDefinedTerm.name: Consensus
hasDefinedTerm.description: Agents vote or debate to reach consensus decisions

hasDefinedTerm.name: Pipeline
hasDefinedTerm.description: Sequential agent chain with output-to-input flow

hasDefinedTerm.name: Hierarchical
hasDefinedTerm.description: Multi-level delegation with manager and worker agents

businessRules.name: AIAgentModelRequirement
businessRules.description: AI agents must specify the AI model they use for reasoning and task execution
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have at least one ea-ai:usesModel relationship to a model in the ModelRegistry

businessRules.name: RAGSystemVectorStore
businessRules.description: RAG systems must reference a vector store for document retrieval functionality
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have a non-empty vectorStore property identifying the vector database

businessRules.name: ResponsibleAIControlCategory
businessRules.description: Responsible AI controls must specify a category for classification and governance
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have a non-empty category property from ea-ai:ResponsibleAICategory

businessRules.name: AIWorkloadResponsibleAICoverage
businessRules.description: AI workloads in production must have at least one Responsible AI control for governance compliance
businessRules.condition: IF ea-ai:AIWorkload is in Production status
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have at least one ea-ai:securedByControl relationship to an ea-ai:ResponsibleAIControl

businessRules.name: MultiAgentOrchestratorRequired
businessRules.description: Multi-agent systems must have an orchestrator for coordination and governance
businessRules.action: THEN there MUST exist an ea-ai:AgentOrchestrator with ea-ai:orchestrates relationships to the participating agents

businessRules.name: ModelRegistryGovernance
businessRules.description: Model registries must track governance level for audit and compliance
businessRules.action: THEN it SHOULD have a non-empty governanceLevel property

businessRules.name: MLPipelineStageDefinition
businessRules.description: ML pipelines must define their stages for reproducibility and auditability
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have a non-empty pipelineStages property

businessRules.name: AIPatternImplementation
businessRules.description: AI workloads should specify which architecture pattern they implement for portfolio analysis
businessRules.action: THEN it SHOULD have an ea-ai:implementsPattern relationship to an ea-ai:AIArchitecturePattern

changeControl.changeNotes: Initial creation. 5 entities extracted from EA-MSFT-ONT v1.0.0 (AIWorkload, AIAgent, RAGSystem, AIArchitecturePattern, ResponsibleAIControl) and made vendor-neutral. 3 new entities added (MLPipeline, ModelRegistry, AgentOrchestrator). Re-namespaced from ea-msft: to ea-ai:. 8 entities, 9 relationships, 8 business rules, 6 enumerations.

versionHistory.notes: Initial release: Vendor-neutral AI/ML ontology extracted from EA-MSFT-ONT. Phase 2 of EA Sub-Series Restructure (Decision D2: Scenario A).

competencyQuestions.question: What AI workloads are deployed across the enterprise and what patterns do they implement?
What AI agents exist and what tools do they have access to?
What RAG systems are deployed and what vector stores do they use?
What Responsible AI controls govern our AI workloads?
How are multi-agent systems orchestrated and what patterns do they follow?
What ML pipelines exist and what models do they produce?
What AI architecture patterns are available and which workloads implement them?
How do Responsible AI controls align to the enterprise security architecture?
